description: Add tight trailing stop after breakeven and fix candle close detection in both standalone EA and Python CandleCloseManager
steps: 2. Fix Python CandleCloseManager candle close detection to use system time instead of relying solely on tick_collector._bar_start_minute
3. Add tight trailing logic to Python CandleCloseManager after breakeven is applied
4. Add input InpTrailDistance to NeuroX_Standalone_v9.mq5 and implement tight trailing after BE
5. Add trailing after BE in Include/NeuroX_Position.mqh ManageOpenPositions()
6. Add tests for tight trailing logic
acceptance_criteria: CandleCloseManager detects candle close using system time (not solely tick_collector._bar_start_minute)
After BE moves SL to lock $5 profit, trailing kicks in at $1 raw price distance behind market
Trail only moves SL in favorable direction (never widens)
Standalone EA has InpTrailDistance=1.00, InpBreakevenProfitThreshold=6.00, InpBreakevenLockAmount=5.00
All existing tests pass, new tests for trailing pass
findings: Python _get_be_sl_price was using BREAKEVEN_LOCK_AMOUNT as raw price offset - fixed to convert dollars to price distance (lockDist = dollars / (lot_size * contract_size))
Floating point: 0.60 * 0.10 * 100 = 5.999... not 6.0 - tests use 0.61 to avoid boundary issue
Added InpBreakevenLock input to bridge EA (NeuroX_EA_v9.mq5) since it was missing

TRAIL FIX: Removed 1-second cooldown from trailing section in both Standalone EA and NeuroX_Position.mqh. Cooldown was blocking rapid SL updates as price moved tick by tick after breakeven. Trail naturally cannot widen (never-widen check stays), so cooldown was unnecessary and harmful.
Added debug Print showing trailSL vs currentSL when trail is blocked (throttled to 1/sec to avoid log spam)
